ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for sakura-flavoured lemon tea with konjac jelly. FOODON_03311737 (tea beverage) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP covers tea-based beverages as a category. No specific code exists for sakura lemon tea with konjac jelly topping.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 16313001 (Tea substance) is the closest available parent. No specific code for flavoured tea beverages with konjac jelly.

main_ingredients: High Mountain Pouchong tea (Bao Zhong oolong)

serving.description: 1 cup (approximately 400 ml with ice and konjac jelly)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 g (one cup), comprising brewed Pouchong tea, fresh green lemon juice, sakura-flavoured konjac jelly pieces, sugar syrup, and ice.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA SR Legacy 14355) and Lemon juice, raw (USDA SR Legacy 09152); Konjac jelly estimated from glucomannan-based jelly analogue; sugar syrup contribution estimated from standard bubble-tea formulation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy); component estimation from peer-reviewed konjac jelly composition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct approved-source entry exists for this specific commercial product. Nutrient values are estimated by component: brewed Pouchong tea approximated from USDA SR Legacy brewed tea (FDC 14355); lemon juice from USDA SR Legacy 09152; konjac jelly from published glucomannan jelly composition (Chua et al., 2010, LWT Food Science and Technology); sugar syrup contribution estimated at 6 g sugar per 100 g final beverage based on standard Taiwanese tea-shop formulation. Values represent a plausible mid-range estimate for a 400 g serving diluted to per-100 g basis.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened tea beverages
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. GI estimated at 35 based on: (1) brewed tea has negligible GI contribution; (2) lemon juice GI approximately 30 (University of Sydney GI Database); (3) konjac glucomannan is known to lower glycemic response (GI contribution near 0); (4) sugar syrup contribution modelled as sucrose GI ~65 at low concentration, blended to composite GI ~35 for the final beverage. This is a conservative analogous estimate consistent with lightly sweetened tea beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per 400 g serving = (35 × 27.2 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 9.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (approximately 3 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: With only ~12 mg sodium per 400 g serving, this beverage poses negligible sodium load and is suitable for individuals managing hypertension, provided added sugar intake is monitored within overall dietary targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from syrup (~6 g sugar per 100 g) and moderate glycemic load (~9.5 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The beverage contains approximately 24 g of sugar per 400 g serving from added syrup; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request reduced-sugar or sugar-free preparation and account for the glycemic load within their daily carbohydrate budget, as the konjac jelly component may attenuate but does not eliminate the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; tea polyphenols may confer cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage is essentially fat-free and cholesterol-free; the Pouchong oolong tea base provides catechins and polyphenols associated with modest cardioprotective effects in epidemiological literature, though the clinical significance depends on overall dietary pattern and consumption frequency.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sakura Lemon Tea with Konjac Jelly (玉露樱花青柠茶) is a Taiwanese-style specialty tea beverage served at CHICHA San Chen, a premium Taiwanese tea-shop chain with an outlet at One Fullerton, Singapore. The drink is built on a base of High Mountain Pouchong (Bao Zhong) oolong tea, a lightly oxidised Taiwanese tea prized for its delicate floral and buttery character. Fresh Taiwanese green lemon (a small, intensely aromatic citrus similar to calamansi) provides bright acidity and citrus zest. Sakura-flavoured konjac jelly pieces — made from glucomannan, a soluble dietary fibre derived from the konjac plant — are added as a topping, contributing a soft, bouncy texture and subtle cherry-blossom aroma. The beverage is typically sweetened with a light sugar syrup and served over ice. Nutritionally, it is a low-calorie, low-fat beverage with approximately 28 kcal per 100 g and around 112 kcal per standard 400 g serving. The sugar content (~6 g per 100 g) is the primary nutritional consideration, particularly for individuals managing blood glucose. The konjac jelly contributes a small amount of soluble fibre (~0.4 g per 100 g), which may modestly attenuate glycemic response. Sodium is negligible. The Pouchong tea base provides tea polyphenols and catechins. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 35 (low), with a glycemic load of ~9.5 per serving. This beverage is vegetarian, vegan, dairy-free, and gluten-free. It is suitable for most dietary patterns when consumed with reduced or no added sugar.

confidence_reason: No direct approved-source nutrient entry exists for this specific commercial product. Nutrient values are estimated by component decomposition using USDA SR Legacy data for brewed tea and lemon juice, supplemented by published konjac jelly composition data and standard Taiwanese tea-shop sugar-syrup formulation assumptions. GI is an analogous estimate anchored to University of Sydney GI Database values for lemon juice and sweetened tea beverages. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are individually well-supported and the estimation methodology is transparent and conservative.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero, as this is a tea-based beverage with no fat-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy brewed tea and lemon juice entries. 2. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are 0.0: naturally zero by the nature of the ingredients (tea, citrus juice, konjac jelly, sugar syrup). 3. The following micronutrients are set to -1 because they are not reported in the primary component sources (USDA SR Legacy for tea and lemon, published data for konjac jelly) and cannot be reliably estimated for this complex beverage without direct laboratory analysis: vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database; selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 4. glycemic_index set to 35 as an analogous estimate; gi_category low. GL per 400 g serving calculated as (35 × 27.2 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 9.5. 5. This product is sold at SGD 7.00 at CHICHA San Chen, One Fullerton, Singapore, sourced from field survey data. 6. The food_name in the input ('Sakura Lemon Tea玉露樱花青柠茶') has been normalised to canonical English 'Sakura Lemon Tea with Konjac Jelly'; the Chinese characters are preserved in local_names and aliases. 7. Dietary flags: vegetarian and vegan status assumed based on ingredient list; halal status assumed as no animal-derived ingredients are present, but not independently verified for this specific outlet. 8. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type throughout as no exact ontology term exists for this specific product. 9. Aliases consistency check (iteration 3): all entries in aliases_by_language including romanized entry 'Yīnghuā qīngníng chá pèi mó yù guǒdòng' have been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
